cards.question: Name the 6 main CSS approaches in React (2026)
cards.answer: Plain CSS/Sass, CSS Modules, Tailwind, Runtime CSS-in-JS, Zero-runtime CSS-in-JS, @scope

cards.question: 6 decision axes for choosing CSS approach in React
cards.answer: Runtime cost, RSC compat, DX, Scoping, Dynamic styling, Bundle footprint

cards.question: Why is runtime CSS-in-JS declining for greenfield React apps?
cards.answer: RSC incompatibility (needs 'use client'), ~14 kB runtime library, CSS strings in JS bundle, SSR hydration issues, no code-splitting

cards.question: What problem does BEM solve in plain CSS React apps?
cards.answer: Namespaces global CSS to prevent naming collisions. Block__Element--Modifier convention. No tooling enforcement — typos produce silent failures.

cards.question: Why is Sass @extend dangerous in React components?
cards.answer: Creates invisible cross-component coupling. Moving/extending a placeholder affects all components using @extend. Removing a stylesheet breaks dependents.

cards.question: How do CSS Modules generate scoped class names?
cards.answer: Build tool transforms .module.css file — '.base' in Button.module.css → '.Button_base_hash'. Import returns object mapping original names to generated names.

cards.question: How do you apply dynamic variants with CSS Modules?
cards.answer: clsx(styles.base, isActive && styles.active, variant === 'danger' && styles.danger). All variants are static CSS — runtime selects via class composition.

cards.question: How does runtime CSS-in-JS inject styles?
cards.answer: Parses tagged template literals in browser → generates unique class name → creates CSS rule string → appends <style> tag to <head>. Every styled component call executes at runtime.

cards.question: Bundle cost breakdown: styled-components vs CSS Modules for 100 components
cards.answer: styled-components: ~14 kB runtime + CSS strings in JS (~30 kB) = ~44 kB gzip. CSS Modules: 0 kB runtime + ~15 kB CSS file = ~15 kB total.

cards.question: How does zero-runtime CSS-in-JS (Vanilla Extract) work?
cards.answer: .css.ts files execute at BUILD time in Node.js. Style objects → static CSS file. Class names replace imports. 0 kB runtime. RSC-compatible.

cards.question: Vanilla Extract recipes vs CSS Modules + clsx — key difference?
cards.answer: Recipes give type-safe variant API — invalid variant = compile error. CSS Modules + clsx same output but no compile-time variant validation.

cards.question: How does Tailwind JIT decide which CSS to generate?
cards.answer: Scans source files for complete utility class strings (e.g., 'bg-blue-500'). Dynamic class names (bg-${color}) may be missed — use safelist.

cards.question: Why do conflicting Tailwind classes need twMerge?
cards.answer: All utilities have equal specificity (one class). 'px-4 px-6' → CSS source order decides winner, not your string order. twMerge resolves by last-in-string-wins logic.

cards.question: 3 override anti-patterns in React component CSS
cards.answer: 1) High-specificity selector stacking (specificity wars). 2) !important (breaks cascade). 3) Deep nesting targeting child internals (coupling to DOM structure).

cards.question: Fix for specificity war: what to use instead of CSS override?
cards.answer: Props over overrides. Component declares variant/size/color props. Consumer uses typed API, not CSS selectors targeting internal DOM.

cards.question: CSS custom property theme architecture: 4 layers
cards.answer: 1) Base :root definitions. 2) Theme variants (.theme-dark overrides). 3) Component tokens (--button-bg: var(--color-primary)). 4) Component implementation (uses var(--button-bg)).

cards.question: Why does CSS custom property theme switching NOT re-render React components?
cards.answer: Changing className on wrapper triggers re-render of only that element. CSS custom properties cascade natively through descendants — browser resolves values without React reconciliation.

cards.question: Flexbox vs Grid — decision rule
cards.answer: 1D alignment (row or column) → Flexbox. 2D (both rows and columns simultaneously) → Grid. Rule of thumb: one direction = Flex, two = Grid.

cards.question: What problem do container queries solve in React?
cards.answer: Component responsiveness independent of viewport. Same component in 4-column grid vs sidebar adapts to available width without variant props.

cards.question: Pattern for unmount animation in React
cards.answer: Set 'exiting' state → apply exit animation className → onAnimationEnd removes element. setTimeout alternative (match animation duration).

cards.question: CSS testing pyramid: layers from bottom to top
cards.answer: TypeScript/lint → Unit tests (variant logic) → A11y audit → Layout tests (breakpoints) → Visual regression (screenshots)

cards.question: What NOT to test in CSS
cards.answer: Browser CSS property rendering (trust spec), class name hashes (implementation detail), third-party CSS, exact pixel values (test visual diff tolerance)

cards.question: When to use clsx vs twMerge vs cva
cards.answer: clsx: universal conditional class merging (228 B). twMerge: Tailwind conflict resolution at component API boundary (6 kB). cva: type-safe variant definitions (1.3 kB).

cards.question: How do CSS Modules automatically code-split?
cards.answer: Bundler assigns per-component CSS to same chunk as the component JS. Lazy-loaded component → its CSS loads only when chunk loads. Runtime CSS-in-JS cannot code-split.

cards.question: Architecture for zero-dependency themable React component library
cards.answer: CSS Modules (scoping) + CSS custom properties (theming tokens) + cva (variant definitions) + twMerge (consumer override). Zero runtime, no mandatory library install.
